metadata.title: Kachemak Bay State Park Hiking Trails
metadata.description: Trail data for 49North Alaska Adventures trail explorer
metadata.source: OpenStreetMap via Overpass API + approximate waypoints

properties.name: Grewingk Glacier Lake Trail
properties.description: The most popular trail in Kachemak Bay State Park. Drop off at Glacier Spit, hike through coastal spruce forest to Grewingk Glacier Lake with floating icebergs. Cross Grewingk Creek via the hand-operated cable tram, then return via the Saddle Trail to water taxi pickup at Saddle Trailhead.
properties.label: Grewingk Glacier Lake Trail | 3.1 mi

properties.name: Alpine Ridge Trail
properties.distance_note: round trip; much longer with approach
properties.trailhead: Halibut Cove area (accessed via Saddle Trail)
properties.description: Ridge trail above Halibut Cove offering panoramic views. Accessed from the Saddle Trail junction. Traverses alpine terrain along the ridge.
properties.label: Alpine Ridge Trail | 2.6 mi

properties.name: Saddle Trail
properties.distance_note: one way, connects Grewingk Lake Trail to Alpine Ridge Trail
properties.description: Connector trail from the Grewingk Lake Trail to the saddle where Alpine Ridge Trail begins. Gains elevation through forest.
properties.label: Saddle Trail | 1.3 mi

properties.name: China Poot Lake Trail
properties.distance_note: one way from Halibut Cove Lagoon
properties.description: Popular trail from Halibut Cove Lagoon Trailhead climbing through forest over a low ridge, passing two small lakes where loons are common and blueberries are plentiful in August. Offers views of Poot Peak before arriving at China Poot Lake.
properties.label: China Poot Lake Trail | 2.6 mi

properties.name: Saddle Trail Junction / Alpine Ridge Trailhead
properties.access: Via Saddle Trail from Grewingk Lake Trail

properties.name: Halibut Cove Lagoon Trailhead
properties.access: Water taxi to Halibut Cove Lagoon

properties.name: Homer Spit
properties.description: Water taxi departure point for Kachemak Bay State Park

properties.name: Grace Ridge Trail
properties.description: Spectacular ridge hike from sea level at Kayak Beach through old-growth Sitka spruce forest to expansive alpine meadows above 3,000 feet. 360-degree panoramic views of Cook Inlet volcanoes, glaciers, and Kachemak Bay.
properties.label: Grace Ridge Trail | 8.1 mi

properties.name: Water Taxi to Halibut Cove Lagoon
properties.label: 10.7 mi | 22-35 min

properties.name: Grewingk Creek Tram
properties.description: Hand-operated cable tram crossing Grewingk Creek. Max 2 persons with packs (500 lbs).

properties.name: Water Taxi to South Grace Ridge
properties.label: 13.8 mi | 29-45 min

properties.name: Emerald Lake Trail
properties.label: Emerald Lake Trail | 1.0 mi
properties.description: Cross Grewingk Creek via the famous hand-operated cable tram, then follow the lakeshore to the tram crossing point. Connects to the Saddle Trail and Humpy Creek Yurt route.

properties.name: Humpy Creek Trail
properties.label: Humpy Creek Trail | 2.3 mi
properties.description: Trail from the Grewingk Creek hand-tram crossing north to the Humpy Creek Yurt. Follows the creek drainage through forest and meadow.

properties.name: Bay Area Tour
properties.description: Scenic boat tour: Homer Harbor out to Gull Island bird rookery, clockwise loop around the island, southeast to Seal Beach (China Poot area, conditions permitting), then return to Homer.
properties.label: Bay Area Tour | Call To Book

properties.name: Water Taxi to Haystack Beach Yurt
properties.label: Call To Book
